column1: content

PRM (Personal Relationship Manager) is a self-hosted app for managing your social network. Core entities are: People (contacts), the Me profile (you), Social Accounts, Relationships, Family connections, Groups, Daily Notes, Notes, Interactions, Images, and Tasks. The app also includes AI features (Ollama chat, semantic vector search, AI event/family generation), facial recognition (PRM-Face), a 3D social graph, a family tree, and gamified ranking tools. This knowledge base explains how to perform each action and where in the UI to do it.

Navigation: the left sidebar (labelled "PRM 2.0") is the primary menu. Top-level items are Home "/home"{Home}, AI Chat "/ai-chat-demo"{AI Chat}, Me "/me"{Me Profile}, People "/"{People List}, Family Tree "/family-tree"{Family Tree}, Groups "/groups"{Groups}, Social Accounts "/social-accounts"{Social Accounts} (with Account Matching "/account-matching"{Account Matching} as a sub-item), Social Graph "/social-graph-3d"{Social Graph}, Daily Notes "/daily-notes"{Daily Notes}, and Games "/games"{Games} (sub-items ELO Ranking and Guess the Sex). A Demos section (Images, PRM Face demos, AI Description) only appears when experimental demos are enabled in settings.

The top header bar contains: a sidebar toggle, the Global Search box in the center, a contextual-actions area, the Universal Add (+) button, a Settings gear "/settings"{Settings}, a theme toggle, and a logout button. On mobile, a bottom navigation bar and a collapsible sidebar footer (Settings, theme, Log out) are used instead.

The Universal Add button is the round + button in the top-right header. Clicking it opens a dropdown to quickly create any of: Person, Social Account, Relationship, Daily Note, Interaction, or Note. Each option opens the corresponding dialog without needing to navigate to a specific page first. This is the fastest way to add a new item from anywhere in the app.

To switch the color theme, click the theme toggle (sun/moon/monitor icon) in the header or sidebar footer. It cycles through three modes: Light, Dark, and System (follows your operating system preference). The choice is saved to your browser's local storage and persists across sessions.

First-time setup: if no account exists the app redirects to the Welcome page "/welcome"{Welcome}, where you create your account (Full Name, Nickname, Username, Password of at least 6 characters). This first account is your "Me" identity. After setup you log in at "/auth"{Login} (or "/auth-direct"{Direct Login}). Logging out uses the logout button in the header/sidebar.

PEOPLE — To see all your contacts, go to the People List at "/"{People List} or "/people"{People}. This is the home/contacts page. From here you can search and filter your contacts and click any person to open their profile.

To add a new person (contact): click the Universal Add (+) button in the header and choose "Person", or use the add control on the People List. Fill the Add Person dialog: First Name and Last Name are required; Email, Phone, Company, Title, Photo, Sex (Unknown/Male/Female), Tags, and a "No social media" checkbox are optional. Click "Add Person" to save.

To edit a person, open their profile at "/person"{Person Profile} (URL needs the person's id/UUID) and click the "Edit" button in the top-right of the header. This opens the same dialog in edit mode where you can change any field, replace the photo, add/remove tags, or set their Sex. Click "Save Changes".

To delete a person, open their profile, click "Edit", then click the red "Delete Person" button at the bottom-left of the dialog and confirm. Deleting a person permanently removes them and all associated data (notes, interactions, relationships); this cannot be undone.

To add a photo to a person, use the Photo upload area at the top of the Add/Edit Person dialog. The image cropper lets you crop to a square. Photos are also used by facial recognition when PRM-Face is enabled.

Tags on a person: in the Add/Edit Person dialog, type a tag in the Tags field and press Enter or click "Add"; remove a tag by clicking the X on its badge. On the person profile, tags appear as chips (PersonTagsChips) below the contact info and can be managed there too.

A person's profile page "/person"{Person Profile} shows a header (avatar, name, title/company, social account chips, email, phone, tags) plus an "Add" dropdown (Relationship, Note, Interaction, and Photo when facial recognition is on), an "Open in graph" button, an "Account info" button (shows created date, ID, ELO score, ELO rankable status, sex), and an Edit button. Below are tabs: Flow, Relationships, Family, Tree, Groups, and Photos.

The Flow tab on a person profile is the activity timeline. It shows the person's notes and interactions together in chronological order. Use the person's "Add" dropdown to add a Note or an Interaction that appears here.

ME PROFILE — Your own profile is at "/me"{Me Profile}. It works like a person profile but represents you. You can be added to relationships and interactions like any other person, and you upload your own photo here. Your personal/login details (name, nickname, username, password, SSO) are edited separately in User Options "/settings/user"{User Options}.

SOCIAL ACCOUNTS — View all linked social media accounts at "/social-accounts"{Social Accounts}. You can search accounts, filter by type (All Types or a specific network), and click an account to open its detail page "/social-accounts"{Social Account Detail} (URL needs the account UUID).

To add a social account, click Universal Add (+) and choose "Social Account", or use "Add Social Account" on a person profile to create and link it in one step. The dialog has two tabs: "Manual Entry" and "XML Import". For manual entry, provide Account URL (the type like Instagram/Twitter is auto-detected from the URL), Username (required), Display Name/Nickname, Account Type, and an optional profile photo. Click "Add Account".

To import a social account from XML: in the Add Social Account dialog choose the "XML Import" tab and upload an Instagram connections.xml file (containing followers and following lists). The import creates accounts and types and skips any that already exist, reporting counts when finished.

To link an existing social account to a person, open the person's profile and use the social account chips area (PersonSocialAccountsChips) or the "Add Social Account" button. Creating an account from a person profile automatically links it to that person.

To edit or delete a social account, open its detail page "/social-accounts"{Social Account Detail} and use the edit (pencil) or delete (trash) buttons. Editing lets you change username, nickname, account URL, type, and profile photo.

A social account detail page shows the profile, an "Account info" panel (account created, first imported, most recent import/action, profile image history), a Followers list, a Following list, a Notes field (free-text notes about the account), and a Posts section. Buttons include "Go to profile" (opens the external URL), "Import from Instagram", "Open in graph", Edit, and Delete.

Scraping/refreshing a social account: use the Scrape / Refresh controls on the account detail page to pull updated data (such as follower counts and profile info). Mass and single-account follower-count refreshes are also available under Tasks settings "/settings/tasks"{Tasks}. Updates run as background tasks tracked in the task list.

To import posts or followers/following for a social account via CSV: on the account detail use the CSV import ("Select CSV File"). When importing follower/following data you choose an Import Type — "Import accounts you follow" or "Import accounts that follow you" — and can set an Override Account.

ACCOUNT MATCHING — Go to "/account-matching"{Account Matching} to link social accounts to the right people. The page presents accounts one at a time with suggested matches; for each you can Match it to a person, Link it, create a new person, or Skip. When everything is linked it shows "All caught up".

RELATIONSHIPS — Relationships are non-family connections between two people (e.g. Friend, Colleague). To add one, click Universal Add (+) > "Relationship" or open a person profile and use Add > Relationship. In the dialog select one or more Related People (you can add several at once), choose a Relationship Type (required), and add optional Notes. Click "Add Relationships".

Your Me identity can be included when adding relationships: if you don't already have a relationship with the person, "Me" appears at the top of the people selector so you can record how you know them.

To edit a relationship, open the person's Relationships tab on their profile, select the relationship, and edit its type or notes. Family-type relationships are managed separately in the Family tab, not here.
The Relationships tab on a person profile lists all of that person's relationships grouped by the other person, showing the relationship type. Use "Add Relationship" to create more.

RELATIONSHIP TYPES — Manage the list of relationship types in Settings > Data Types "/settings/data-types"{Data Types}, on the "Relationship Types" tab. Each type has a Name, a Color, a numeric Value (1-255, used for weighting/strength, default 50), and optional Notes. Add a type with "Add Type", click a type to edit it, and use the X to delete it. Deleting a type removes its reference from existing relationships.

FAMILY — Family connections (parents, spouses/partners, children, siblings) are managed on a person profile's Family tab, separate from regular relationships. The tab shows three sections: Parents, Spouses & Partners, and Children, each with an Add button.

To add a family member, open the Family tab and click Add Parent/Partner/Child. In the dialog pick a Connection Type (Parent, Spouse/Partner, Child, or Sibling), then either "Link existing person" (search and select) or "Create new person" (enter first name, last name, sex). For parent/child/sibling links choose a Lineage Type: Biological, Adoptive, or Step. For spouses choose a Relationship Status: Married/Spouse, Partner/Significant Other, Divorced/Ex-Spouse, or Ex-Partner.

When adding a spouse/partner, you can also define that new partner's relationship to the person's existing children (Not connected, Biological parent, Step-parent, or Adoptive parent), which creates the corresponding lineage links automatically. Adding a Sibling links the new person to the existing parents (or creates a placeholder parent if none exist).

To edit or remove a family connection, open the Family tab, hover the member card and use the menu (three dots) to "Edit Connection" (change lineage type or partnership status) or "Remove Link". Family changes also update the Family Tree.

AI FAMILY GENERATION — You can describe a person's family in plain language and let AI propose the connections. Open the Generate Connections dialog (Sparkles "Generate connections"), type a description (e.g. "I am Sam Smith, my brother is Mark Smith, my dad is Dale Smith"), optionally enable "Willing to delete connections?" and "Ask about changes?", then click "Generate changes". Review the proposed add/edit/delete changes (each with a checkbox and reason), deselect any you don't want, and click "Apply" to commit only the approved changes.
GROUPS — View all groups at "/groups"{Groups}. Groups organize contacts (e.g. Marketing Team, Family, Book Club). Click a group to open its profile "/group"{Group Profile} (URL needs the group id).

To create a group, click Universal Add (+) is not used for groups; instead use the add control on the Groups page. In the Add New Group dialog set a Group Name (required), a Group Color (color picker or hex), optional Group Types (free-text tags like Team/Project, add with Enter), an optional group image, and select initial Members from your people list. Click "Create Group".

To edit a group, open it and edit its name, color, types, or image. Members are managed on the group's Members tab (not in the edit dialog when editing). To delete a group, use the delete control and confirm ("Delete Group").
A group profile has tabs including Members (add/remove people) and Interactions. You can add an interaction directly to a group, which records it against all members.

DAILY NOTES — Journal entries by day live at "/daily-notes"{Daily Notes}. Each day is one note; click a day to open its detail "/daily-notes"{Daily Note Detail} (URL needs the note id/date). The list shows your notes with a "Today" shortcut.

To create or edit a daily note, click Universal Add (+) > "Daily Note" or open a day. The modal has a Title (optional), a Body written in Markdown (with an Edit/Preview toggle), an Events list, and Involved Parties. Click Save to store it.

Daily note Events: add structured event rows to a note with "Add event" (or press Enter in a field). You can also click "AI generate" to have the AI extract a list of events from the note body automatically. Remove an event with its trash icon.

Daily note Involved Parties: tag who/what a day involved using the "Add" party picker — you can attach People, Groups, and Social Accounts. These appear as badges and link the note to those entities. Remove a party with the X on its badge.

Locking a daily note with a PIN: notes can be made non-editable/locked. A locked note shows a lock icon and is read-only until you enter its PIN ("Enter PIN to Edit"). You can set or change a note's PIN (4-8 characters) from the note detail. Provide the current PIN to unlock and the new PIN to change it.

Vectorizing daily notes: enable Vector Storage in settings to semantically search your notes. Once enabled you can vectorize all notes and then find them by meaning via Super Search. See Vector Storage settings "/settings/vector"{Vector Storage}.

NOTES — A Note is a free-text note attached to one person (distinct from Daily Notes). Add one via Universal Add (+) > "Note", or from a person profile Add > Note. Enter the note content (required) and optionally attach an image. Notes appear on the person's Flow tab.

INTERACTIONS — An Interaction records an event between people (e.g. a meeting or call). Add one via Universal Add (+) > "Interaction", from a person profile (Add > Interaction), or from a group. In the dialog set an optional Title, a Type (required), a Date/time, an optional Description, select at least 2 people involved, optionally select involved Groups, and optionally attach an image. Click "Add Interaction".

Interactions require at least two people. When opened from a person or group profile, that person/group is pre-selected. Interactions show on the Flow tab of each involved person and on group profiles.

INTERACTION TYPES — Manage interaction types in Settings > Data Types "/settings/data-types"{Data Types} on the "Interaction Types" tab. Each type has a Name, Color, numeric Value (1-255, default 50), and optional Description. The "Generic" type is protected and cannot be deleted. Deleting a type removes its reference from existing interactions.

SOCIAL ACCOUNT TYPES — Manage the available networks (Instagram, Twitter/X, TikTok, etc.) in Settings > Data Types "/settings/data-types"{Data Types} on the "Social Account Types" tab. Each type has just a Name and Color. Add, edit (click the type), or delete (X) types. Deleting a type removes its reference from existing social accounts. Account URLs are auto-matched to these types when adding accounts.

GLOBAL SEARCH — The Search box in the header (placeholder "Search...") does a quick lookup across your data as you type. Use "Search Settings" to adjust the Search Order (which entity types are prioritized). Results let you jump directly to people, accounts, groups, and more.

SUPER SEARCH — For AI-powered semantic search across everything, go to Super Search "/super-search"{Super Search} (placeholder "Search across all your data with AI..."). It uses vector embeddings to find relevant people, notes, and interactions by meaning rather than exact keywords. Requires Vector Storage to be enabled and content to be vectorized.

AI CHAT — Chat with an AI assistant about your network at "/ai-chat-demo"{AI Chat}. Type a message (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a newline). Use Chat Settings to pick a Model and set a System message. You can branch off a conversation into a new chat (choosing a model), edit prompts, and the assistant can call tools (with approval) to read your PRM data. Requires AI/Ollama to be enabled and configured.

AI tool approvals: when the AI tries to use a tool, a Tool Approval popup asks you to allow or deny it. You control which tools the AI may call in Settings > Intelligence > Tools "/settings/intelligence/tools"{AI Tools}.

FAMILY TREE — The interactive family tree is at "/family-tree"{Family Tree}. It renders people and their parent/child/partner links as a visual graph. Click a node to see Person Info (relationships, root of tree). You can add a new family relationship directly in the tree (choose a relationship type) and there is a Development Version for advanced/experimental layout work. The tree reflects family connections made on person Family tabs.

SOCIAL GRAPH (3D) — The 3D social graph is at "/social-graph-3d"{Social Graph}. It visualizes people and/or social accounts and their connections. Open the Graph Options panel to choose the Graph Mode, adjust Blob Size Multiplier/Force, highlight specific accounts (type 3+ characters to search), toggle "Show Friend Links", "Remove Extras", "Hide Orphans", set Minimum Connections and Max Extras, and configure Node Colors (by Follow Status, Account Type) and Line Colors (Mutual, Follows you). A Hybrid people+accounts view is marked coming soon.

To open a specific person or account in the graph, use the "Open in graph" button on their profile, or pass URL parameters (e.g. ?view=person&selected=PERSON_ID). The older "/graph"{2D Graph} and "/graph-3d" routes redirect into the social graph. Default graph behavior is configured in Settings > App Options > Social Graph "/settings/social-graph"{Social Graph Settings}.

IMAGES — Browse uploaded photos at "/images"{Images} (under Demos when experimental features are on). Click a photo to open its detail "/image"{Image Detail} (URL needs the image id), which shows Links, Location, and recognition data. Images are also attached to notes, interactions, and people.

PRM-FACE (FACIAL RECOGNITION) — PRM-Face detects and matches faces in your photos. When enabled, a Photos tab appears on person profiles and you can add face photos via the person's Add > Photo menu. Try it on the demo pages "/prm-face-demo"{PRM Face Demo} and "/prm-face-save-demo"{PRM Face Save Demo}. Configure the PRM-Face API server URL, API key/setup code, and toggle features in Settings > Recognition "/settings/recognition"{Recognition}. Sub-page Recognition Faces "/settings/recognition/faces"{Recognition Faces} manages detected faces.
AI DESCRIPTION DEMO — Generate AI descriptions of images at "/ai-desc-demo"{AI Description Demo} (Demos section). Requires AI to be enabled.

GAMES — Game-style tools live under "/games"{Games}. ELO Ranking "/elo-ranking"{ELO Ranking} shows you two contacts at a time and you pick a winner; this updates each person's ELO score and leaderboard (needs enough rankable people, otherwise it shows "Not enough people"). Guess the Sex "/guess-the-sex"{Guess the Sex} is a guessing game over your contacts. A person's ELO score and whether they are "Rankable" are visible in their Account Info panel.

SETTINGS — Open Settings via the gear icon in the header "/settings"{Settings}. Settings has its own sidebar: User Options, App Options (Social Graph, Chrome Extension, API Settings, Experimental Features), Data Types, Image Storage (Storage, Image Table), Intelligence (Tools, External Tools, Images, Family Tree, Vector Storage), Tasks (Image Tasks), Import & Export (Contacts, Social Media, Application Data, Image Pass In, Instagram XML Transfer), Recognition (Images, Faces), API Documentation, and Delete Options. "Back to Site" returns to the main app.

USER OPTIONS "/settings/user"{User Options} — Update your personal information and login: Nickname, Username, SSO Email (optional, for SSO login), and change your password (Current, New, Confirm). It also has an SSO Configuration section: Enable SSO, Auto SSO, Client ID, Client Secret, Auth URL, Access Token URL, User Info URL, and Redirect URL for OAuth single sign-on.
APP OPTIONS "/settings/app"{App Options} — General application preferences. Sub-pages include Social Graph defaults, Chrome Extension, API Settings, and Experimental Features.

EXPERIMENTAL FEATURES "/settings/experimental"{Experimental Features} — Toggle experimental/in-progress features, including enabling the Demos section in the main sidebar (Images, PRM Face demos, AI Description) and other optional UI like the Photos tab.

DATA TYPES "/settings/data-types"{Data Types} — One page with three tabs to manage Relationship Types, Interaction Types, and Social Account Types (see those sections for fields). This is where you customize the categories used throughout the app.

INTELLIGENCE (AI) "/settings/intelligence"{Intelligence} — Configure the Ollama LLM backend: Enable AI, set the Ollama API/Server URL (e.g. http://localhost:11434), optional HTTP Basic Auth (Username/Password), and Verify Connection. This powers AI Chat, AI event generation, AI family generation, and AI descriptions.

AI TOOLS "/settings/intelligence/tools"{AI Tools} — Enable "AI tool calls" so the assistant can query your PRM data, and enable the "app knowledge base" (this CSV) so the AI can answer how-to questions about the app. Shows Knowledge Base Stats (Total chunks, Vectorized, Pending) and lets you re-index it. External tool integrations are configured at "/settings/intelligence/external-tools"{External AI Tools}. AI image and family-tree intelligence have their own pages "/settings/intelligence/images"{AI Images} and "/settings/intelligence/family-tree"{AI Family Tree}.

VECTOR STORAGE "/settings/vector"{Vector Storage} — Configure the Qdrant vector database: Enable Vector Storage, Enable vector sync, set the Qdrant Server URL (e.g. http://localhost:6333), Collection name (e.g. prm_daily_notes), and Embedding Model. Shows Total notes / Vectorized / Pending counts. "Enable universal vectorization" (Vectorize Everything) indexes all entity types for semantic Super Search, with a progress indicator.

IMAGE STORAGE "/settings/image-storage"{Image Storage} — Choose the Storage Mode for images, view Image Statistics, and Transfer/backfill images between storage backends (runs as a background task). The Image Table sub-page "/settings/image-storage/table"{Image Table} lists stored images for inspection/management.

TASKS "/settings/tasks"{Tasks} — Manage background workers and jobs. Available tasks include Single Account Follower Count Refresh (search an account, 3+ characters) and Mass Follower Count Refresh. You can start/cancel tasks, pause/resume the worker, remove all tasks, and view Task History. Image-specific tasks are under "/settings/image-storage/tasks"{Image Tasks}. Open a task's detail at "/settings/task"{Task Detail} (needs task id). Completed XML exports notify you with a toast and a download button appears in the task list.

SOCIAL GRAPH SETTINGS "/settings/social-graph"{Social Graph Settings} — Set the graph's Default Mode (Default or Single), Filters (Hide orphans, Minimum connections, Limit extras, Max extras), Default Mode Colors (color scheme: By type / By distance / By connection count), and Single Highlight Mode options (default account, node color scheme, follow status colors). These become the defaults when you open the Social Graph.

API SETTINGS "/settings/api-settings"{API Settings} — Create and manage API Keys for external/programmatic access. Click "Create API Key", give it a Key Name (e.g. "Mobile App"), and copy the generated key (shown once). Full endpoint reference is in API Documentation "/settings/api"{API Documentation}.

CHROME EXTENSION "/settings/chrome-extension"{Chrome Extension} — Install and connect the PRM Chrome extension so it can talk to your PRM instance. Shows Active Sessions and lets you Revoke an extension session. The extension helps capture social profiles/connections from the browser.
DELETE OPTIONS "/settings/delete"{Delete Options} — Bulk/dangerous data deletion controls for clearing categories of data. Use carefully; deletions are generally irreversible.

IMPORT & EXPORT — The hub is at "/settings/import-export"{Import & Export}. From here you can import contacts, import social media data, export/import the whole application database, pass in images, and transfer Instagram XML.

IMPORT CONTACTS "/settings/import-export/contacts"{Import Contacts} — Import contacts from a CSV (First Name and Last Name are required; Tags/Labels are parsed automatically) or from a VCF (vCard) file. VCF files can contain one or many contacts.

IMPORT SOCIAL MEDIA "/settings/import-export/social-media"{Import Social Media} — Import social media connections from a CSV. You can set an Override Account (search 3+ characters) and Manual Overrides to control how rows map to accounts.

APPLICATION DATA EXPORT/IMPORT "/settings/import-export/application"{Application Data} — "Export All Data" downloads your entire PRM database as XML (note: images are not included). "Import from XML" restores from such a file; duplicate IDs are skipped. Useful for backups and moving between instances.
IMAGE PASS IN "/settings/import-export/image-pass-in"{Image Pass In} — Bulk-provide images into the app for processing/recognition.

INSTAGRAM XML TRANSFER "/settings/import-export/instagram-xml"{Instagram XML Transfer} — Load an Instagram XML transfer file and map its accounts (search usernames or nicknames) by Username, Nickname, Network, and Action Status to move follower/following data into PRM.

API & AUTOMATION — PRM exposes a REST API documented at "/settings/api"{API Documentation}; authenticate with API keys created in API Settings. Background processing (scraping, follower refresh, image transfer, exports, vectorization) runs through the task worker, monitored in Tasks settings and the floating Task Tracker modal.

COMMON ID REQUIREMENT — Several detail routes need an entity's id/UUID in the URL: person "/person/:id", group "/group/:id", social account "/social-accounts/:uuid", image "/image/:id", daily note "/daily-notes/:id", and task "/settings/task/:id". You normally reach these by clicking an item in its list rather than typing the URL, since the id is filled in automatically.

QUICK TASK INDEX — Add a contact: Universal Add > Person. Add a relationship: Universal Add > Relationship or person Add menu. Add family: person profile Family tab. Add a social account: Universal Add > Social Account. Create a group: Groups page. Write today's journal: Universal Add > Daily Note. Log a meeting: Universal Add > Interaction. Manage categories: Settings > Data Types. Find by meaning: Super Search. Visualize network: Social Graph or Family Tree. Configure AI: Settings > Intelligence.
